title: 'Standards should not be lowered': How Tharoor reacted to Kharge's Gujarat remark
content: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge's remarks about Kerala voters being "educated and clever" and unlike those in Gujarat have drawn sharp criticism from the BJP. Thiruvananthapuram MP Shashi Tharoor responded by urging a higher standard of political discourse, stating that "our standards should not be lowered to that of those who use bad language."

title: TN polls: AIADMK’s Leema Rose Martin is the richest candidate with Rs 1,049 crore assets
content: Leema Rose Martin, AIADMK's Lalgudi candidate, is the wealthiest contender in the upcoming Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. She declared assets totaling approximately Rs 1,049.56 crore. Her affidavit reveals significant movable and immovable properties. Martin's husband and son also have substantial declared assets. The candidate faces pending criminal cases, some related to tax and money laundering investigations.

title: Google, OpenAI & Anthropic come together to fight Silicon Valley's 'Chinese problem'
content: Three of America's biggest tech rivals—OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google—are sharing intelligence through the Frontier Model Forum to combat distillation attacks from Chinese AI labs. Anthropic has named DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax as having run industrial-scale extraction campaigns against Claude using 24,000 fraudulent accounts. Both companies have flagged the threat to US lawmakers, framing unauthorized distillation as a national security risk, not just a commercial one.

title: PSL scare: Pakistan player loses consciousness after head blow, taken to hospital
content: The right-arm medium-fast bowler was taken to a nearby hospital for checks. The Geo Super report said that Mir Hamza Sajjad is now conscious and stable. As a precaution, a CT scan will be done to check for any injury.

title: Canada has changed immigration rules; changing visa fee, work permit rules and more
content: Canada's immigration landscape is shifting from April 2026 with passport and citizenship fee increases, offset by a 30-day processing guarantee. Super visa rules are relaxed, allowing sponsors more flexibility. Provinces gain increased autonomy in immigration assessments, while rural employers benefit from expanded foreign worker quotas.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' collection day 20: Ranveer Singh film surges past Rs. 1,025 crore
content: Aditya Dhar’s 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has scripted history as the first Hindi film to breach the Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone. Globally, the thriller has amassed Rs. 1,622.72 crore, outperforming blockbusters like Jawan. Despite the IPL 2026 season and a 229-minute runtime, the film remains a powerhouse. To boost family viewership, makers have released updated prints with muted abuses and technical fixes. Now the third-highest-grossing Indian film globally, the spy saga eyes the Rs. 1,700 crore mark.

title: NHL insider Craig Button says Toronto Maple Leafs GM search tied to Auston Matthews future
content: The Toronto Maple Leafs are about to have a very important offseason, during which they will make decisions about their leadership that will affect their future. Craig Button, an NHL insider, says that the search for a new general manager for the team will be closely linked to Auston Matthews' future. The organization has to make important decisions about its roster direction, coaching stability, and contract dynamics. All of that could change the way it competes.

title: Sam Altman shares 13-page-long vision for a world where AI beats human intelligence
content: OpenAI has unveiled ambitious policy proposals for a future shaped by superintelligent AI. The company suggests significant shifts in taxation, worker benefits, and government programs. These ideas aim to address potential economic changes as AI advances. OpenAI's plan includes new tax structures and support for workers. The proposals also touch upon public investment funds and workforce transitions.

title: Kittle linked to Cowboys move as 49ers eye $5M defensive upgrade
content: Jadeveon Clowney is a highly rated edge defender. He last played for the Dallas Cowboys. He is a potential target for the San Francisco 49ers. Their roster has injured players and the farewell of Bryce Huff. Clowney’s beyond impressive 2025 stats and PFF grades could bolster the Niners’ weakest in the league pass rush. However, he told KPRC 2 that he wants a reunion with Lamar Jackson in Baltimore.

title: Rory McIlroy marriage drama deepens as book reveals divorce twist
content: Rory McIlroy’s short-lived divorce from Erica Stoll in 2024 drew huge attention, especially with Amanda Balionis rumors. A new book by Alan Shipnuck shares inside details, but confirms no affair. The couple reunited within a month, calling it a “wake-up call.” Despite personal challenges, McIlroy continues to focus on golf with family support.

title: Joey Barton assault case: Kevin Lynch may lose eyesight in one eye
content: Former footballer Joey Barton has been remanded in custody after an alleged assault on Kevin Lynch at Huyton and Prescot Golf Club in Liverpool. Lynch suffered serious facial injuries and may lose sight in one eye. Barton and Gary O’Grady face grievous bodily harm charges. On the same day, a court also ordered Barton to pay damages to Eni Aluko.

title: Centre asks states to double daily supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders for migrant workers
content: States and Union Territories are directed to double the daily supply of 5-kg LPG cylinders for migrant workers, ensuring targeted distribution with Oil Marketing Company support. The Centre assures a stable overall LPG supply, dismissing reports of shortages at petrol pumps and LPG distributors.  Over 6.75 lakh 5-kg cylinders have been sold since March 23.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equity benchmarks, Sensex and Nifty, staged a strong intraday recovery to close higher on Tuesday. Easing crude oil prices, positive global cues, and robust buying in IT stocks fueled the rebound. Despite initial weakness, markets ended firmly in the green, with IT stocks leading the gains.

title: Firing outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul; 1 gunman killed, 2 in custody— video
content: Gunfire erupted outside Istanbul's Israeli consulate as three assailants attacked, resulting in one gunman killed and two apprehended. Turkish authorities, suspecting links to a religion-exploiting terror group possibly affiliated with IS, are investigating. The consulate had been vacant for years, and no Israeli personnel were present. Two police officers sustained injuries during the intense shootout.

title: Gold price today: Yellow metal slips; check 24K, 22K city-wise rates
content: Gold futures dipped Tuesday as rising crude oil prices and Middle East tensions made investors cautious, impacting demand for the safe-haven asset. While futures saw a decline, retail gold rates presented a mixed picture across major Indian cities, with some experiencing slight increases and others minor drops.

title: Tamim Iqbal replaces Bulbul as BCB chief amid push for better BCCI ties
content: The current president of BCB, Aminul Islam Bulbul was removed by the country's National Sports Council (NSC), which formed an ad-hoc committee to run the day-to-day affairs till fresh elections are held.

title: David Warner arrested for drunk-driving in Australia
content: Australian cricketer David Warner faces serious charges after being apprehended for drunk-driving in Sydney. Pulled over by police, the 39-year-old's breath test allegedly registered a reading of 0.104, significantly exceeding the legal limit. He is now scheduled to appear in court next month following his arrest.

title: Crores paid for IPL, commitment missing? Gavaskar's savage swipe at overseas stars
content: Cricket legend Sunil Gavaskar has called for stricter BCCI action against IPL players not performing their roles. He questions workload management excuses for all-rounders not bowling. Gavaskar also revealed foreign cricket boards earn significant revenue from player No Objection Certificates for the IPL. This system is unique to the IPL globally.

title: 'Disturbed, desperate, rattled': Congress vs Himanta over raids at Pawan Khera’s residence
content: Congress intensified its attack on Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma after police searched Pawan Khera's Delhi home. The action follows a defamation FIR by Sarma's wife over passport allegations. Congress leaders decried the move as a 'witch hunt' and misuse of state power, demanding a probe into Sarma's wife's alleged foreign assets and passports.

title: 'Everyone is trying to squeeze something out of you!' Techie compares life in Mumbai and Bengaluru
content: An IIT Bombay graduate's observation about Bengaluru's interactions feeling 'businesslike' and 'full of negotiations' sparked a viral debate comparing it to Mumbai's perceived easegoing nature. While some agreed, others cautioned against generalizations, highlighting Bengaluru's diversity and rapid growth. The discussion underscores how personal expectations and economic factors shape urban cultural experiences.

title: Scientists find promising new drug against rare, life-threatening kidney disease
content: A groundbreaking oral drug, N-propargylglycine (N-PPG), has shown remarkable success in preventing fatal kidney damage in mice with Primary Hyperoxaluria Type 2 (PH2). This rare genetic disorder causes excessive oxalate buildup, leading to kidney failure. The drug effectively halts stone formation and protects kidneys, offering significant hope for a new treatment and potentially reducing the need for transplants.

title: Rift in INDIA bloc? Congress slams Mamata’s collusion charge
content: Congress MP Manickam Tagore criticized West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee for her "irresponsible allegations" hinting at collusion between the BJP and INDIA bloc allies. Tagore stressed the need for opposition unity against the BJP, reminding the TMC of its past alliance with the BJP and highlighting Rahul Gandhi's fight against the saffron party.

title: ‘Dhoni’s absence doesn’t make much difference’: SRH star’s candid take on CSK
content: Sunrisers Hyderabad batter Heinrich Klaasen believes Chennai Super Kings remain a formidable force despite the absence of MS Dhoni as a player. Klaasen suggested Dhoni's impact is more in leadership than his limited batting contributions, emphasising CSK's overall strength. He expressed confidence in SRH's ability to perform despite a slow start to IPL 2026.

title: Stock market today: Nifty50 ends up at 23,123; Sensex climbs 500 points to 74,616
content: Dalal Street opened higher on Tuesday, with benchmark indices rising after a strong rebound in the previous session. Intense buying in banking and IT stocks, coupled with a strengthening rupee, boosted investor confidence. Global markets showed a mixed trend as oil prices climbed amid Middle East tensions.

title: Amid ‘Dhurandhar 2’, recalling when Ram Gopal Varma said that Dawood had a cold
content: Filmmaker Ram Gopal Varma revealed his 'humanly' approach to gangster characters in films like 'Satya' and 'Company'. He explained that insights from police and a reporter showed even notorious figures like Dawood Ibrahim experienced vulnerability, such as getting a fever or a cold, humanizing them beyond their perceived fear factor.

title: Mounting losses, cost pressures: What may have led to Air India CEO Campbell Wilson’s exit
content: Campbell Wilson is stepping down as Air India's CEO and Managing Director. The Tata Group carrier announced his departure on Tuesday. Wilson will remain until a successor is found. This move comes as Air India navigates financial challenges and a gradual revival post-privatisation. A panel is now searching for his replacement.

title: 'The kala in his hand': How Mukesh Kumar became Delhi Capitals' go-to bowler
content: Mukesh Kumar has emerged as an unlikely yet vital weapon for Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026, silencing doubters with discipline and control. Backed by the team management, the Bengal pacer has relied on hard lengths and consistency to deliver.

title: 'Already fled to Hyderabad': Himanta mocks Khera as police search his Delhi residence
content: Assam Police searched Congress leader Pawan Khera's Delhi residence following an FIR by Assam CM Himanta Biswa Sarma's wife over passport allegations. Khera had claimed Sarma's wife held three passports, a charge denied by the BJP. The Chief Minister vowed strong action, accusing Congress of using unauthorized documents and alleging Pakistani links.

title: 12m barrels of crude! India ramps up Venezuela oil imports to 6-yr high amid US-Iran war
content: India, which relies on imports for about 90% of its crude oil needs, has been exploring alternative sources after the conflict affected flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a route that typically handles nearly 40% of its oil supplies.

title: Baisakhi celebrations: Pakistan grants over 2,800 visas to Indian pilgrims
content: Over 2,800 Indian Sikh pilgrims are set to visit Pakistan for Baisakhi celebrations, exploring revered sites like Gurdwara Panja Sahib and Nankana Sahib. This annual journey, facilitated by a 1974 protocol, underscores Pakistan's dedication to fostering interreligious harmony and goodwill. Officials expressed hopes for a spiritually enriching experience for all attendees.

title: Way-Too-Early Top 25: New national champ Michigan opens at No. 1
content: The 2026 champion is crowned. We're rolling right into ranking next season's best teams -- with a few new rules.

content: Here's how next season is taking shape for every team that made the final rounds of the NCAA tournament.

title: Michigan hits 'ultimate target,' wins NCAA title
content: Michigan won its second national title by becoming the first team since 2009 to beat UConn in the Sweet 16 or later.

title: Hurley, Karaban reflective after UConn bows out
content: In their last game together, UConn coach Dan Hurley and Huskies star Alex Karaban saw a bid for a third national title in four years come to an end at the hands of the Michigan Wolverines, who produced a 69-63 victory at Lucas Oil Stadium on Monday night.

title: Contreras warns Brewers: Hit me again, you'll pay
content: Willson Contreras had a warning for the Brewers after he was hit by a pitch for the 24th time by the team Monday. "... Next time they hit me again, I'm going to take one of them out. That's a message."

title: Bears HOFer McMichael, who died at 67, had CTE
content: Steve 'Mongo' McMichael died in 2025 at age 67 after a five-year battle with ALS, and his wife Misty donated his brain to research.

title: Grizzlies tie NBA mark for 3s with 29 but still lose
content: The Grizzlies tied the NBA single-game record with 29 3-pointers on Monday but still lost 142-126 at home to the Cavaliers.

title: Lady Vols lose recruit Edwards, see roster cut to 1
content: On the same day that Tennessee guard Jaida Civil announced she's entering the transfer portal, Oliviyah Edwards, the No. 2 recruit in the SC Next 100 class of 2026, requested a release from her national letter of intent with the Lady Volunteers on Monday.

title: Cooper irked as penalty box injury sparks probe
content: The NHL is looking into the circumstances that led to Tampa Bay Lightning forward Pontus Holmberg hurting his left arm when he crashed shoulder-first into an unlatched penalty box door in Monday night's 4-2 loss to the Buffalo Sabres at KeyBank Center.

content: North Carolina intends to hire former longtime NBA coach Michael Malone as the school's next basketball coach, sources told ESPN.

content: The long-pending project to divert water from the Lokapavani river to local tanks has now been completed and formally dedicated after several years, says Mr. Bommai

content: The projects have the potential to create 1,11,278 jobs; CM calls for more investments in renewable energy and the development of strong transmission infrastructure

content: The Andhra Pradesh Teachers Federation claimed that the introduction of a tablet-based application for recording marks is causing operational difficulties, resulting in delays and additional workload for teachers engaged in valuation

content: When Mr. Sengottaiyan’s nomination papers were taken up, representatives of AIADMK candidate V.B. Prabhu raised objections, pointing to alleged discrepancies in the affidavit.

title: Kalpakkam reactor’s milestone a proud moment for India, says A.P. Chief Minister Chandrababu Naidu
content: The successful attainment of criticality by the Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor marks a key step in the second stage of India’s nuclear programme, bringing the country closer to harnessing its vast thorium reserves and strengthening its long-term energy security, he says

content: Foreign policy is not about short-term optics or daily narratives; it must be assessed over the long term. Pakistan's repeated attempts to project itself as a decisive global player must be seen in this context.

content: The elephant turned aggressive while being led for the Sheeveli ritual at the Thadukkassery Nagankulangara Temple

content: Over 1,700 dairy farm families spread over Karnataka, Telangana and Tamil Nadu, that supply milk to Akshayakalpa, an organic milk player, have installed biogas plants. The families generate more biogas than they need for their kitchen.

content: The officials said Special Intensive Revision (SIR) could be rolled out after the polls conclude on April 29; another possibility is to begin the massive exercise after the declaration of results.

content: Villagers, who had earlier resolved to boycott the upcoming Assembly election, had put up banners at prominent places stating that no political party should enter the village in Erode district for vote-seeking.

title: Watch: Sabarimala case: How did it begin and where is it now?
content: The Supreme Court of India has now begun final hearings in the long-running Sabarimala case. A nine-judge Bench led by Chief Justice Surya Kant is hearing a batch of petitions that go beyond just one temple. At the core, the court is looking at a much larger question. To what extent can courts intervene in matters of religion? And what exactly counts as an essential religious practice under Article 25 of the Constitution?

title: EV retail sales in India up 24.6% at 24.52 lakh units in FY26: FADA
content: While electric two-wheelers crossed 14 lakh units, electric passenger vehicle retail sales missed the two lakh units by a whisker but surged 83.63% in FY26, FADA said

title: SC hearing on Sabarimala women entry highlights: Legislature, not courts, entitled to enact social reform, says SG

title: Everything that is at stake in the final week of the regular season
content: seeding races in both the East and the West are beginning to heat up . five teams in the West have guaranteed their spots in the playoffs . the Oklahoma City Thunder clinched the division for the third season in a row .

title: Masters 2026: Ranking all 91 players in the field
content: for the first time in his career, Rory McIlroy will drive down Magnolia Lane this week as a Masters champion . a year ago, he made a 4-foot birdie putt on the 18th hole to defeat england's Justin Rose on the first hole of a sudden-death playoff . with his long-awaited Masters victory, Mcilroy became only the sixth golfer to complete the career Grand Slam .
